title: Authorization / Access Denied
description: The user is missing one or more authorization objects required to perform the action.
steps: Run SU53 as the affected user (or ask them to run it) immediately after the error to capture the failed authorization check.
Note the missing authorization object, field, and value from the SU53 output.
Go to PFCG and find the role(s) assigned to the user.
Add the missing authorization to the appropriate role and regenerate the profile.
Re-assign the role to the user in SU01 if needed (profile regeneration usually suffices).
Test access again.

title: Transport Imported but Change Not Visible
description: A transport was imported successfully but the expected changes are not visible in the target system.
steps: Check the import log in STMS — look for warnings or errors that may have caused partial import.
Verify the transport was imported to the correct client.
Check if the object was locked or overwritten by a later transport.
For Customizing transports, confirm the correct client was targeted.
If the import log shows success, check whether the change requires activation (e.g., ABAP programs need activation).
Re-import the transport if needed using STMS import queue.

title: OData Service Not Found / Not Active
description: An OData service returns a 404 or is not accessible. Usually caused by the service not being activated or a cache issue.
steps: Go to /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and search for the service. Check if it is active.
If not active, add and activate the service.
Check SICF to ensure the ICF node for the service is active.
Run /IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP on the backend to clear the metadata cache.
Run /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP on the Gateway frontend.
Test the service URL again from a browser or Postman.

title: Background Job Failed or Aborted
description: A scheduled background job has failed, aborted, or did not run as expected.
steps: Open SM37 and filter for the job by name, user, or date range. Check the job status.
Open the job log to see the last message before termination.
If the log shows an ABAP exception, go to ST22 to view the short dump.
Check SM21 system log for related system-level errors at the time of failure.
Identify whether the issue is a data problem, a missing authorization, or a program error.
Fix the root cause and reschedule the job.

title: ABAP Runtime Error / Short Dump
description: An ABAP program has terminated with a runtime error (short dump). Common causes include type conflicts, null pointer access, or missing customizing.
steps: Go to ST22 and find the dump by date, time, user, or program name.
Read the exception description and the active call stack.
Identify the program and line where the error occurred.
Check if it is a standard SAP program — if so, check OSS notes.
If it is a custom program, involve the developer to fix the code.
If the dump is authorization-related, check SU53.

title: Timesheet Entry Missing or Not Saved
description: A user's timesheet entry is not visible or appears to not have been saved in CATS.
steps: Open CAT2 and check the entry for the relevant person and date.
Verify the entry was saved and released — unsaved entries are lost.
Check if the entry was transferred to HR/Payroll via the transfer background job in SM37.
Use CAT3 to display the time sheet for the employee in display mode for confirmation.
If entries are systematically missing, check the CATS transfer job in SM37.

title: Workflow Stuck or Not Progressing
description: A workflow instance is not advancing, usually because a workitem has no agent or the responsible user cannot process it.
steps: Go to SWI5 and analyze workitems by task or agent to find the stuck item.
Check whether an agent is assigned — if not, the workitem has no one to action it.
Use SWIA to execute or forward the workitem administratively.
Check the workflow log to understand where the process stalled.
If systematically recurring, review the role resolution for the task in the workflow definition.

title: User Account Locked
description: A user cannot log in because their account is locked, either administratively or due to failed logon attempts.
steps: Go to SU01, enter the username, and click Display or Change.
On the Logon Data tab, check the lock status.
If locked by failed logon attempts, unlock the account.
If administratively locked by another admin, investigate the reason before unlocking.
Check SM21 system log for logon failure patterns if repeated lockouts occur.
Advise the user to reset their password after unlocking.
